_note: Sector correlation library for tradebrain. Leader = ticker that moves first and drags the sector. Beta = rough move multiplier vs leader. Correlated_sectors = sectors that tend to move together.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - compute
sectors.leader_note: NVDA is the anchor for the entire AI ecosystem. NVDA earnings/guidance moves the whole sector.
sectors.risk_note: NVDA earnings weeks cause IV spikes across entire sector. Avoid buying options into NVDA earnings unless trading the event itself.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - memory
sectors.leader_note: MU leads the memory cycle. Currently rotating ahead of GPU stocks as HBM demand tightens supply.
sectors.risk_note: Memory cycle is currently decoupled from GPU cycle - MU up does not always mean NVDA up. Treat as separate trade.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - equipment
sectors.leader_note: AMAT and LRCX lead equipment. ASML is the global monopoly on EUV lithography - its moves signal the entire fab buildout.
sectors.risk_note: Equipment stocks lag compute by 1-2 quarters. Positive NVDA guidance = future AMAT order tailwind, not immediate move.

sectors.name: AI infrastructure and data centers
sectors.leader_note: NBIS leads the high-beta AI infra cluster. CRWV and APLD are the next highest beta. All share power/energy cost as primary driver.
sectors.risk_note: Energy prices are a direct cost driver - oil/gas down = AI infra up. This cluster moves 2-3x the broader market on risk-on days. Very high IV, wide spreads. Size carefully.

sectors.name: Big Tech - hyperscalers
sectors.leader_note: MSFT leads because Azure/OpenAI capex signals the demand side for the entire AI ecosystem. GOOGL and META follow. AAPL is more defensive and less correlated to AI.
sectors.risk_note: Hyperscaler capex guidance (Azure, AWS, GCP growth %) is the single most important data point for the entire AI trade. Watch quarterly earnings closely.

sectors.name: Consumer retail
sectors.leader_note: WMT leads consumer retail sentiment. COST and TGT follow. Amazon is retail but trades as big tech. Primary drivers are consumer spending data, CPI, tariffs, and earnings.
sectors.risk_note: Consumer spending data, CPI prints, and tariff headlines are primary catalysts. Tariffs hit margins directly — any trade war escalation moves this sector hard. Options liquidity is good on WMT, COST, HD. TGT spreads can be wide.

sectors.name: AI software and productivity
sectors.leader_note: PLTR leads sentiment for the AI software category. CRM and SOUN follow. This sector lags the hardware cycle by 2-4 quarters.
sectors.risk_note: AI software stocks are sentiment-driven and can disconnect from fundamentals. High short interest on most names = squeeze risk on positive catalysts.

sectors.name: Cloud and cybersecurity
sectors.leader_note: CRWD leads the cyber cluster. PANW is the large-cap anchor. S (SentinelOne) is the AI-native challenger. ZS and FTNT follow. Cyber is more defensive than pure AI plays.
sectors.risk_note: Cybersecurity is defensive — tends to hold up better in risk-off moves. Budget cycles and breach headlines are primary catalysts. S specifically has had fundamentals-vs-price disconnect — buy thesis needs a catalyst. LAES is micro-cap — options spreads will be very wide, treat as 1 contract max.

sectors.name: Real estate and proptech
sectors.leader_note: Zillow leads proptech sentiment. OPEN is the highest-beta name — pure iBuyer model with direct housing market exposure. All move on Fed rate decisions, mortgage rate data, and housing inventory prints.
sectors.risk_note: Fed rate decisions are the single biggest driver — rate cuts = sector rip, rate hikes = sector crush. Housing inventory data and mortgage application weekly prints are leading indicators. OPEN is the most volatile — high IV, wide spreads, size at 1 contract max.

sectors.name: Industrial automation and robotics
sectors.leader_note: Robotics is early-stage with no dominant leader yet. PATH and SYM are the most liquid. SERV is speculative.
sectors.risk_note: Very thin liquidity on most names. Options spreads are wide. Stick to PATH and SYM if trading options - avoid SERV options entirely.

sectors.name: Defense and aerospace
sectors.leader_note: RTX leads defense sentiment. ONDS and RCAT are the speculative drone plays — high beta to defense spending news and conflict escalation.
sectors.risk_note: Defense moves on geopolitical headlines and government budget news. Drone names (ONDS, RCAT) are high-beta speculative plays within the sector — size small.

sectors.name: Healthcare and healthtech
sectors.leader_note: HIMS leads the high-growth healthtech cluster. OSCR follows. UNH is a large-cap traditional insurer - inverse correlation to HIMS on policy news.
sectors.risk_note: UNH and HIMS can move inversely on healthcare policy news. Treat as separate trades. HIMS is a momentum name - high IV, moves fast.

sectors.name: Entertainment and media
sectors.leader_note: NFLX leads streaming sentiment. DIS follows with theme park and studio exposure. Both move on subscriber data and content pipeline news.
sectors.risk_note: Subscriber growth and content spend are primary drivers. Streaming wars compress margins. DIS has theme park exposure which adds consumer spending sensitivity.

sectors.name: Crypto miners and power plays
sectors.leader_note: MARA leads crypto miner sentiment. RIOT follows. BITF is more leveraged to BTC. All three correlate with BTC price AND power costs - same dual driver as AI infra.
sectors.risk_note: BTC price is the primary driver. Secondary driver is power costs and regulatory news. Very high IV - buying options is expensive. Consider selling premium instead.

sectors.name: Quantum computing
sectors.leader_note: IONQ is now the most liquid and institutionally backed quantum name. RGTI and QBTS follow. All move on quantum news and NVIDIA comments about AI+quantum convergence.
sectors.risk_note: Pure speculation tier. Revenue is real but small. Move on press releases, conference mentions, and big-tech quantum comments. IONQ is the most liquid - use it as the proxy for the others. Never more than 1 contract. Treat as lottery tickets with a real thesis.

sectors.name: Broad market ETFs
sectors.leader_note: SPY is the macro anchor. QQQ leads tech. IWM leads small caps and is a risk appetite indicator. When IWM lags QQQ, risk appetite is narrowing.
sectors.risk_note: QQQ/SPY ratio is a useful tech sentiment gauge. QQQ outperforming = risk-on tech. QQQ underperforming = rotation to value.

sectors.name: Banks and financials
sectors.leader_note: JPM leads bank sentiment. BAC and WFC follow. Rate decisions and yield curve are primary drivers.
sectors.risk_note: Rate sensitive — Fed decisions move the whole group. Trade around FOMC meetings and earnings.

sectors.name: Travel and leisure
sectors.leader_note: BKNG leads online travel sentiment. ABNB follows. Both move on travel demand data, consumer spending strength, and summer/holiday booking trends.
sectors.risk_note: Consumer spending and travel demand are primary drivers. Macro slowdown fears hit this sector hard. Summer booking data and airline capacity are leading indicators.

sectors.name: Energy infrastructure - new
sectors.leader_note: SMR leads the nuclear/clean energy plays for AI power demand. EOSE is the battery storage angle. FCEL is the fuel cell play. All benefit from data center power demand and clean energy buildout.
sectors.risk_note: Nuclear and energy storage plays benefit from the same AI power demand thesis as data centers. Less volatile than AI infra - better for defined-risk plays.

sectors.name: Fintech and financial platforms
sectors.leader_note: COIN leads fintech sentiment. HOOD follows retail trading volume. SOFI is rate-sensitive.
sectors.risk_note: Sensitive to crypto prices, interest rates, and retail trading volume

sectors.name: EV and Auto
sectors.leader_note: TSLA leads EV sentiment globally. RIVN and NIO follow with higher beta. GM/F are traditional auto proxies with lower beta.
sectors.risk_note: EV names are macro-sensitive and policy-driven. Tariff news, EV incentive changes, and TSLA delivery data are primary catalysts. NIO and RIVN have thin margins — any guidance miss hits hard.

sectors.name: Social media and consumer internet
sectors.leader_note: META leads social/consumer internet. SNAP and PINS are high-beta followers. Ad market conditions drive the whole group.
sectors.risk_note: Ad spend is cyclical — recession fears compress the whole group fast. SNAP is the highest beta name relative to META. Earnings weeks cause IV spikes across the cluster.

sectors.name: China ADRs
sectors.leader_note: BABA leads China ADR sentiment. PDD and JD follow. Geopolitical headlines and US-China trade policy are the primary macro drivers.
sectors.risk_note: China ADRs can gap 5-10% on single headlines — tariffs, delisting threats, regulatory crackdowns. Always check geopolitical calendar before entering. High IV is normal — options are expensive.

sectors.name: Traditional energy
sectors.leader_note: XOM leads traditional energy. OXY and CVX follow. Oil price and production data are the primary drivers.
sectors.risk_note: Oil macro drives the sector — WTI price, OPEC decisions, inventory data. Lower volatility than AI names. Better for defined-risk plays with wider DTE.

sectors.name: Space and satellite
sectors.leader_note: RKLB leads the commercial space cluster. ASTS is the satellite connectivity play. Both move on launch success/failure news and SpaceX competition.
sectors.risk_note: Pure speculation tier. Moves on launch events, contract wins, and SpaceX competition headlines. RKLB is the most liquid — use as proxy. Wide options spreads expected.

cross_sector_signals.note: NVDA up = entire AI stack bid. Strongest same-day correlation to AMD, AVGO, ARM. Data center stocks follow within 1-2 sessions. NVDA earnings week = IV spike across entire sector.
MSFT Azure guidance = demand signal for entire cloud and AI software stack. Software names follow within 1-2 sessions.
Broad market risk-on lifts high-beta AI names disproportionately. QQQ typically leads SPY on tech-driven days. VIX below 15 = risk-on, high-beta names outperform.
AI data center cluster moves together on energy cost and hyperscaler demand news. NBIS leads — all others follow same session.
MU leads memory cycle — HBM demand signal. Semi-decoupled from GPU compute — treat as separate rotation.
TSLA leads EV sentiment globally — RIVN and NIO follow same/next session on delivery data and EV policy news.
COIN leads crypto fintech sentiment — retail crypto trading volume and BTC proxy. All follow on BTC price moves.
MARA leads crypto miners — all follow BTC price same session. Secondary driver is power costs.
IONQ leads quantum computing cluster — all move on quantum news same session. NVDA quantum comments are the biggest catalyst.
RKLB leads commercial space — satellite and space infra follow on launch success and contract news.
RTX leads defense sentiment on geopolitical headlines — drone names follow with higher beta. Conflict escalation = whole group bid.
SMR leads nuclear theme — small cap nuclear names follow same session. AI power demand and energy security are shared catalysts.
BABA leads China ADR rotation — all move on US-China trade headlines same session. Tariff news moves whole cluster instantly.
JPM earnings lead bank sector — results set the tone for the group within hours. Rate guidance is the key signal.
XOM leads traditional energy on oil price moves — WTI up = whole group bid same session.
FSLR leads solar/clean energy cluster — IRA policy news and energy independence theme move the group together.
PLTR leads AI software sentiment — smaller AI software names follow with 1-2 session lag. Government AI adoption is the shared catalyst.
CRWD leads cybersecurity — breach headlines and IT budget cycle news move whole cluster. Cyber is defensive — holds better in risk-off.
AMD rips → rotation into value semi names. QCOM and DELL follow 1-2 sessions later. MI300 GPU data center wins are the catalyst.
AMZN consumer/AWS strength signals broader e-commerce and consumer platform names. AWS growth = cloud sector bid.
META ad revenue beat signals ad market health — social media cluster follows same session. Ad spend cycle drives whole group.
NOW leads enterprise software rotation — CRM and TEAM follow. IT budget cycle and enterprise AI adoption are shared drivers.
SNOW leads data/analytics cluster — PLTR and IBM follow. Data platform spend and AI training data demand are shared catalysts.
HIMS leads growth healthtech — OSCR follows on health platform sentiment. GLP-1 and telehealth policy are key catalysts.
Rate-sensitive fintech cluster moves together — Fed pivot signals move all three. Rate cut = whole cluster rips.
SPOT consumer discretionary strength signals broader consumer rotation into SHOP and LULU. Premium consumer spend is the shared driver.
ARM architecture wins drive royalty beneficiaries — QCOM, AVGO, MRVL all benefit from ARM adoption across mobile and AI chips.
TSM monthly revenue is the leading indicator for entire semi complex — signals fab demand 1-2 quarters ahead. Watch mid-month revenue print.
